justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Three distinct integration patterns clearly identified and designed — SAP batch/SFTP with outbox pattern, DHL REST/webhook with store-and-forward, Analytics Kafka streaming. (2) DHL 500ms constraint explicitly addressed via Webhook Ingress Gateway that ACKs immediately and queues for async processing. (3) DHL webhook idempotency addressed with deduplication via event ID in Redis/DB and idempotent consumer design. (4) SAP silent failure risk identified — outbox pattern ensures failed batches are retried, orders only marked exported after SFTP confirmation, dead letter with alerting. (5) Dedicated integration layer recommended — each external system has its own ACL adapter, domain services have zero knowledge of external systems. (6) Flexibility points explicitly cover adding new carriers (e.g., FedEx) via new adapter with no changes to internal systems.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough three-pattern decomposition with correct protocol-level details for each (SFTP/XML batch, REST/webhook async, Kafka streaming)
Webhook Ingress Gateway as a separate component from DHL Adapter is a sophisticated design choice that cleanly separates the fast-ACK concern from processing
Outbox pattern for SAP batch is the right choice — ensures no silent data loss between event emission and SFTP upload
Idempotency addressed at multiple levels: webhook ingress deduplication key, adapter-level idempotent status application, and general pitfall warning
Risk analysis is comprehensive — covers DHL duplicates, SAP silent failure, event bus SPOF, Kafka backpressure, schema drift, and webhook security
Implementation guidance includes phased approach and explicit pitfalls-to-avoid that map directly to the scenario's failure modes
Domain model with bounded contexts and context map shows DDD-level thinking appropriate for the complexity

agent_output_excerpt:
{
  "executive_summary": "Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, and a Kafka-based Analytics Platform) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per external system. The design isolates each integration behind clear boundaries, ensures the 500ms webhook SLA via async processing, and provides a single internal event stream that feeds all downstream consumers including
